text: Artificial intelligence is shaping many parts of our daily lives, from job applications to medical diagnoses. However, this technology also raises important ethical questions. One major concern is bias. If an AI system is trained on biased data, it can make unfair decisions, such as favouring certain groups over others. This directly opposes the principle of fairness, which should guide all AI use. Another key issue is privacy. Many AI tools collect large amounts of personal data, so users need to understand how their information is used. Transparency is also essential—people should know when and why an AI is making a decision. To address these challenges, responsible AI development must focus on creating systems that are ethical, accountable, and built for the benefit of everyone.
